title: Likelihood-ratio inference on differences in quantiles
summary: Quantiles can represent key operational and business metrics, but the computational challenges associated with inference has hampered their adoption in online experimentation. In a new paper, I present a two-sample difference-in-quantile hypothesis test and confidence interval based on a likelihood-ratio test statistic. It can be computed using only four order statistics from each sample. arXiv link: Likelihood-ratio inference on differences…

title: Attention Is Off By One
summary: Transformer has a mathematical bug that has been overlooked for 6+ years. I propose fixing its outliers with two new devices, Softmax One and QuietAttention: Attention Is Off By One

title: Formulas for Bootstrapping Sample Medians
summary: A paper from Spotify on quantile estimation has been making the rounds. I take a stab at their critical conjecture, and uncover a few Formulas for Bootstrapping Sample Medians.

title: You Can’t Spell CUPED Without Frisch-Waugh-Lovell
summary: A/B tests run faster with CUPED. Here I explain the underlying math, and use it to invent an even better variance-reduction technique for online experiments: You Can’t Spell CUPED Without Frisch-Waugh-Lovell

title: The Floppy Disk of Floating Point
summary: An essay that bids farewell to x87, a computing architecture too long for this world: The Floppy Disk of Floating Point

title: Updated: Formulas for Bayesian A/B Testing
summary: I’ve updated Formulas for Bayesian A/B Testing to include a new equation for analyzing four-pronged (A/B/C/D) tests.

title: SlowerLogLog
summary: What rolls down stairs and counts unique items imprecisely? HyperLogLog. Also SlowerLogLog

title: Preface to A Lost Lady
summary: A Lost Lady, a Willa Cather novel which entered the public domain this year, is a gentle study in decline on the former frontier. Read my Preface to A Lost Lady

title: Preface to Brave New World
summary: Brave New World reacquainted political philosophy with the prurient, after a two thousand year hiatus. Read my Preface to Brave New World

title: Preface to The Time Machine
summary: The Time Machine is my favorite science fiction novella. What makes it special? Read my Preface to The Time Machine

title: Preface to Frankenstein
summary: Frankenstein turned two hundred last year. Why is it still relevant? My very own Preface to Frankenstein

title: The Amex EveryDay Bonus: A Stochastic Valuation Model
summary: How much is the EveryDay Bonus from American Express worth? A probabilistic investigation: The Amex EveryDay Bonus: A Stochastic Valuation Model

title: Things That Bother Me About Swift
summary: I’m learning Swift. Some minor grievances I have with the language: Things That Bother Me About Swift

title: Things That Bother Me About macOS
summary: In honor of the upcoming Festivus, I am sharing some Mac-related grievances: Things That Bother Me About macOS

title: A Review of Perl 6
summary: I spent a few weeks with Perl 6. Here’s what I think: A Review of Perl 6

title: Why I’m Learning Perl 6
summary: The Perl 6 virtual machine is a promising piece of technology: Why I’m Learning Perl 6

title: Adventure Games and Eigenvalues
summary: Identifying and quantifying dead ends, the bane of all adventure game players: Adventure Games and Eigenvalues

title: Big Tablets Are Coming
summary: Reflections on the Microsoft Surface Studio and Apple’s inevitable giant iPad: Big Tablets Are Coming

title: Type Punning Functions in C
summary: Making C more flexible, with the help of x86_64 calling conventions: Type Punning Functions in C

title: Elixir RAM and the Template of Doom
summary: A systems-y look at HTML template rendering: Elixir RAM and the Template of Doom

title: Splatoon’s Ranking System Is Still Broken
summary: Nintendo keeps trying to fix Splatoon’s ranking system. They won’t succeed without a solid understanding of linear algebra. Read more: Splatoon’s Ranking System Is Still Broken

title: Simple Sequential A/B Testing
summary: Cut your A/B sample sizes in half, using this one weird trick: Simple Sequential A/B Testing

title: Updated: Formulas for Bayesian A/B Testing
summary: I’ve added several new formulas to “A Formula for A/B Testing” (now known as Formulas for Bayesian A/B Testing). In addition to including the count data formula on the page, I have extended both formulas to work with three-pronged tests, that is, A/B/C tests.

title: Evaluating Splatoon’s Ranking System
summary: Evaluating Splatoon’s Ranking System — Using probability theory to uncover a subtle bug in Splatoon.

title: Inferring Tweet Quality From Retweets
summary: Inferring Tweet Quality From Retweets — Modeling tweets, retweets, and the passage of time.

title: Ranking News Items With Upvotes
summary: Ranking News Items With Upvotes — A formula for social news websites.

title: Deriving the Reddit Formula
summary: Deriving the Reddit Formula — What can expected-utility theory tell us about Reddit’s “hot” formula?

title: A Taste of Rust
summary: A Taste of Rust — I tried Rust, and it was better than eating a bucket of nails.

title: Four Days of Go
summary: Four Days of Go — If you see the Go gopher, tell him I would like my pajamas back.
